fault_hypothesis: Line 73 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so when `now == effective` the entry is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

spec.expr: not (now == effective__1) or (now >= effective__1 and __cbfl_result is None)

constraints.intent: When now equals effective, the function must return None (entry is expired); the strict > comparison fails this at the boundary.
After computing effective, verify that the subsequent comparison will correctly treat now >= effective as expired; placeholder to anchor near the fault.
Immediately before effective__1 is used in the comparison, assert that equality of now and effective implies the entry should be expired (now >= effective must trigger expiry).
In the else branch (entry returned as valid), now must be strictly less than effective; if now == effective we should not reach here.
